item.title: How much interest can you earn with a 4.20% CD account now?
item.description: It's easy to find a CD account with an interest rate of 4.20% now. Here's how much interest that translates to.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire over, "going to hit them hard again tonight"
item.description: Trump said he's not sure he wants to make a deal with the Iranians, but also said he doesn't think the war will "start again."

item.title: White House announces "Freedom Fuel" gas station selling $3.47 gasoline
item.description: The Freedom Fuel Network is offering gasoline for more than 30 cents a gallon below the national average.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will give Ukraine right to make Patriot missiles
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday that the U.S. will grant Ukraine's request for a production license to build Patriot missile interceptors for defense. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $10,000 18-month CD will earn savers who act now
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD of this size and length will be substantial, but that's not the only benefit for savers.

item.title: ICE agent fatally shoots man in Houston
item.description: An investigation is underway after an ICE agent shot and killed a man during a targeted enforcement operation in Houston, Texas. CBS News' Shawna Mizelle reports.

item.title: Parents of football player who died speak out after charges filed against ex-coach
item.description: Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday announced that his office is charging former Bucknell coach Mark Kulbis with felony aggravated hazing.

item.title: DOGE shuts down after controversial run with Elon Musk
item.description: DOGE, the government agency once led by Elon Musk which canceled billions in public contracts and sent scores of federal employees packing, is shutting down. CBS News Mornings anchor Michael George reports.

item.title: Maine Democratic Party accuses Platner team of interfering with process to pick his replacement
item.description: Pressure continues to mount for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner to end his campaign after a former girlfriend accused him of sexual assault. Now, the Maine Democratic Party is accusing Platner's camp of interfering with the process to select a new nominee if he does drop out. Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Judge orders release of more than $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump sex abuse, defamation case
item.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

item.title: Details on the situation in Iran as Trump threatens more strikes
item.description: President Trump is threatening more strikes against Iran after saying the ceasefire is over. Robert B. Murrett, retired vice admiral in the U.S. Navy, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Children who died on Wisconsin's Geneva Lake found in sunken boat, police say
item.description: Three children who died when a boat capsized on Geneva Lake in Wisconsin amid a sudden storm last week were found inside the sunken vessel, police said.

item.title: Filed your FAFSA? Here are 3 other ways student loan borrowers can get help for fall 2026
item.description: Federal aid may not cover your full college tuition bill, but there are ways to close the gap before classes begin.

item.title: Watch: Trump slams Iran, calls regime "scum" and says he's unsure about deal as NATO summit wraps
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday spoke to reporters about a number of topics as the NATO summit in Turkey came to an end. At one point, Mr. Trump said he wasn't sure if he wants to make a deal with Iran and threatened new strikes against the nation. Watch the full press conference.

item.title: Argentina rallies to win against Egypt in stunning 3-2 comeback World Cup match
item.description: Reigning champion Argentina pulled ahead against Egypt in the Round of 16, scoring a spot in the FIFA World Cup quarterfinal. Former U.S. Men's National Team player Marcelo Balboa shares his analysis.

item.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
item.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list
item.description: The nominations for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards were announced Wednesday morning in Los Angeles, with the final season of HBO Max's "Hacks" setting a new record for the most nominations in a single year for a comedy series.

item.title: Sneak peek: My Mother's Murder Trials
item.description: ENCORE: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports in a two-part "48 Hours" airing Saturday, July 11 from 9-11 p.m. ET on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Trump says he's not sure he wants to make a deal with Iran
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday said he wasn't sure he wants to make a deal with Iran and threatened more strikes against the country. CBS News' Nancy Cordes reports and Robert B. Murrett, professor of practice at Syracuse University, joins with analysis.

item.title: Trump hails "unity" at NATO summit, despite recent tensions
item.description: President Trump concluded the NATO summit with a press conference touting the "tremendous love" exhibited by the allies.

item.title: El-Sayed, Stevens clash over Israel and campaign money in Michigan Democratic Senate debate
item.description: Progressive former health director Abdul El-Sayed and centrist Rep. Haley Stevens debated as the two vie for the lead in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary. Nikole Killion has the latest.

item.title: Democrats call for Platner to exit Maine Senate race as new allegations emerge
item.description: Despite growing calls for Senate hopeful Graham Platner to step aside, the Maine Democrat has only denied recent abuse allegations against him. High-profile Democrats are now urging Platner to exit the race as a key election deadline looms. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Nolan Wells' family seeks answers after missing Mississippi teen found dead
item.description: The family of Nolan Wells, who was found dead after a July 4 boat trip to Horn Island, has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump.

item.title: Toddler declared dead in pool accident found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: Two police officers saw possible signs of life, but the child was still taken to the hospital's "cold room" after being treated by staff, according to police documents.

item.title: Why do airlines make passengers check their carry-on at the gate?
item.description: Catching a flight with just a carry-on can make travel feel easy. No need to check a bag and all your belongings stay with you. But quite often, that suddenly changes before boarding.

item.title: Ex-Bucknell University coach charged in death of freshman football player
item.description: A former Bucknell University coach has been charged in the death of a freshman football player who collapsed during practice and died in 2024.

item.title: 7/7: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: More Democrats call for Graham Platner to end his campaign; U.S. launches new strikes against Iran over attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Rahm Emanuel expected to say Israel needs "significant changes" in speech
item.description: Rahm Emanuel​ is viewed as a potential Democratic presidential candidate and has been a longtime defender of Israel.

item.title: Boomer Parents: It's Time to Cough it Up | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
item.description:
Americans have been anxiously awaiting the so-called "great wealth transfer," where trillions of dollars are expected to pass down from Baby Boomers to their kids. But people are living longer and holding onto their assets. Jill says it's time to start opening up the purse strings now. Plus, caller questions: Toby asks whether sterling silver jewelry is actually a worthwhile investment, and Dave wants to know if taking a pay cut to improve his quality of life is financially realistic.
Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.
